data.executive_summary:
Net revenue reached $87,561, achieving 97.3% of goal with strong performance driven by neuromodulator services generating $67,557 and representing 64.1% of total service revenue. The practice demonstrated exceptional marketing efficiency with a lead-to-booking conversion rate of 42.5%, nearly triple the 15% target, and achieved a first-visit ROI of 7.6x against a 3x goal. Membership growth remained robust with 178 new members added and a low churn rate of just 1.5%, contributing $11,855 in monthly recurring revenue.

The clearest opportunity to support even more consistent growth lies in client retention, with the 180-day retention rate currently at 26%, well below the 65% target. Strengthening follow-up protocols and client engagement strategies could help convert the strong new client acquisition into lasting relationships that drive recurring revenue.

Performance varies significantly across the team, with Mandy Haug operating at maximum capacity with 99% utilization and generating $53,412 in net revenue at a $441 average order value. Meanwhile, several providers including Kenna Salas at 64% utilization and Sandy Schaffer at 51% present opportunities to balance appointment distribution and maximize the team's collective revenue potential while ensuring sustainable scheduling for high-performing providers.

assessments.title: Strong Utilization Performance
assessments.text: Utilization at 73.3% is well above the 60% benchmark, indicating effective capacity management. This solid foundation supports consistent revenue generation.

assessments.title: Marketing ROI Success
assessments.text: First-visit ROI at 7.6x significantly exceeds the 3x goal, with lead-to-booking rate at 42.5% versus 15% target. Current marketing investments are generating strong returns.

assessments.title: Low Client Retention
assessments.text: 180-day retention at 26% is well below the 65% target, indicating clients are not returning for follow-up treatments. Focus on post-treatment communication and rebooking protocols.

assessments.title: Revenue Goal Within Reach
assessments.text: At 97.3% of revenue goal, the practice is $2,439 short of target. Converting additional leads from the strong marketing pipeline could close this gap.

assessments.title: Rebooking Rate Below Target
assessments.text: Rebooking rate at 33% falls short of the 60% benchmark. Implementing checkout rebooking protocols and treatment plan discussions could improve client continuity.

data.psm_feedback:
**Your team acquisition strength is wasted on one-time transactions rather than building lasting relationships.** With 76% new clients but only 26% retention at 180 days, you're operating more like a high-end day spa than a medical practice. The numbers show clients value what you offer—your $406 first-visit AOV and 7.6x marketing ROI prove demand exists—but they're not staying long enough to see real results or justify your acquisition costs. Focus your April training on treatment planning conversations that position services as progressive journeys rather than standalone visits.

**Mandy's production at 99% utilization and $441 AOV demonstrates your service model works, but five other providers sitting below 65% utilization represent untapped capacity.** The revenue gap is significant: Kenna's $155 AOV suggests she's primarily handling less complex services, while Sandy, Jenna, Alex, and Hailey combine for under $2,100 in total production. Rather than hiring additional staff, redistribute your new client flow to push these providers toward 70% utilization and implement AOV coaching focused on service upgrades and proper treatment intervals.

**Your rebooking rate of 33% directly correlates to your retention problem and represents the most controllable revenue lever you have.** With 174 total appointments, moving rebooking from 33% to 50% would generate an additional 30 pre-scheduled visits monthly at your current $354 AOV—roughly $10,600 in additional revenue without any marketing spend. Train your team to present rebooking as treatment protocol completion rather than optional scheduling, and require a specific next appointment recommendation before any client leaves the chair.

**Marketing performance at 42.5% lead-to-booking conversion and $19 cost per lead shows your front-end systems are working, making retention the priority over acquisition expansion.** Your current $1,502 monthly ad spend is generating sufficient new clients, but with 74% of those clients not returning by month six, you're essentially funding a competitor's future customer base. Implement a 30-60-90 day automated follow-up sequence for new clients and shift marketing budget toward retention tools like email campaigns for existing clients before increasing acquisition spend.
data.marketing_recommendations:
**Performance Summary**
Marketing delivered a **7.6x ROI** in March, significantly above the 3x goal. With $1,502 in ad spend, the campaigns generated 80 new patient leads and **$11,366 in attributed revenue**. The **$19 cost per lead** and **$406 first-visit AOV** both performed well within target ranges.

**Funnel Analysis**
The conversion funnel shows strong performance at both stages, with leads converting to bookings at **42.5%** (well above the 15% goal) and booked appointments completing at **82.4%**. The booking-to-completion gap represents the larger opportunity—6 patients who scheduled but didn't show. **Closing this completion gap** could have added approximately $2,400 in additional revenue for the month.

**Actionable Next Steps**
Implement **appointment confirmation calls** 24-48 hours before scheduled visits to reduce the 18% no-show rate among new patients. Consider adding **SMS reminders** with easy rescheduling options to capture patients who might otherwise cancel last-minute. Test offering **new patient incentives** that require completion of the first visit to redeem, creating additional motivation to follow through on bookings.

launches.title: Client tags
launches.description: Client tags give you and your practice high-level, at-a-glance context on every client right where you need it. You can add and remove tags directly from a client's profile, edit existing tag names and icons, create new custom tags with unique icons, and see client tags throughout your workflow from scheduling to invoicing.

launches.title: Payroll sync with time tracking
launches.description: Employee hours logged through Time Tracking can now sync directly into Payroll, eliminating the need to manually enter worked hours. This update removes one of the most time-consuming parts of payroll processing, resulting in a cleaner operational workflow and fewer errors.

launches.title: Online booking intervals
launches.description: You can now define the booking interval for your online booking calendar, which controls the appointment start times clients see when scheduling online. This gives you greater control over how appointments are scheduled, helping reduce awkward gaps in your calendar and align online bookings with your real-world workflow.

launches.title: Automatic terminal selection
launches.description: If your medspa has multiple payment terminals connected, Moxie Suite will now automatically select the last terminal used during your next in-person payment. This update helps reduce friction during busy checkout moments and keeps your team moving faster.

launches.title: Inventory brand management
launches.description: You can now view and edit product brands directly within your inventory for both service and retail products. Easily track products by brand, improve reporting visibility, and keep your inventory organized, especially helpful for practices managing multiple product lines.
